description: Major Mesoamerican deities with visual attributes for generative AI. Each entry describes how to visually construct the deity figure.

deities.name: Quetzalcoatl

visual_attributes.serpent_form: A rattlesnake body covered in quetzal feathers; iridescent green feathers replace scales
visual_attributes.human_form: Buccal (duck-beak) wind mask; conical straw hat (ehecailacacozcatl); cut shell cross-section (ehecailacacozcatl) pectoral
visual_attributes.architectural_form: Circular temples (Ehecatl/Quetzalcoatl shrines have rounded plan); serpent-column bases at Tula and Chichen Itza

deities.generative_hint: Feathered serpent: undulating sinuous body; feathers shown as overlapping U-chevrons along dorsal surface; head with open fanged jaw and feather collar; iridescent green dominant. For wind-god human form: duckbill mask, conical hat, shell cross-section pectoral.

deities.name: Tlaloc

diagnostic_elements: goggle eyes (circular rings around both eyes)
visual_attributes.teotihuacan_form: Frontal; large circular goggle eyes; water droplets and shells surround figure; often in register bands with repeated identical forms
visual_attributes.aztec_form: Profile (in codices); goggle eyes; horizontal upper lip element; nose-ornament; often blue body paint
visual_attributes.maya_chac_form: Hook/scroll nose (curved beak-like extension); single large eye; forked lightning tongue; axe in hand; Puuc architecture: stacked Chac masks at corners

deities.generative_hint: Goggle eyes = the defining mark: two concentric circles for each eye. In 3D/sculptural contexts, the goggle eye ring is a physical projecting element. In 2D codex: two solid circles. Upper lip = two parallel horizontal curves or interlocked serpents. For Maya Chac: exaggerated downward hook nose (single curved scroll); large circular eye; no goggles.

deities.name: Huitzilopochtli

visual_attributes.weapon: Xiuhcoatl (turquoise/fire serpent) as a long weapon-staff with serpent head
visual_attributes.headdress: Hummingbird leg extending from headdress; quetzal feathers

deities.generative_hint: Blue-black body paint as the primary surface color. Solar disk elements in the composition. The fire serpent weapon curves forward from the right hand. Rarely seen in sculpture (no major surviving sculpture confirmed); primarily codex form.

deities.name: Tezcatlipoca

diagnostic_elements: obsidian smoking mirror replacing left foot
turquoise mirror on temple or chest
visual_attributes.smoking_mirror: Circular obsidian disk with smoke scrolls rising from it; replaces left foot; sometimes worn on chest or temple
visual_attributes.body_paint: Horizontal black and yellow horizontal stripes; or solid black with yellow star spots

deities.generative_hint: The smoking mirror is the diagnostic: a circular obsidian disk (very dark, near-black with slight reflective quality) with smoke scrolls rising from it. The left foot is replaced by this mirror. Body: black+yellow stripes. Jaguar skin associations = spot overlay.

deities.name: Coatlicue

diagnostic_elements: head replaced by two facing serpent heads (forming a face)
necklace of hands, hearts, and skull pendant
visual_attributes.composition: Frontal; massive blocky form; the entire figure is layered with symbolic elements; no empty surface

deities.generative_hint: A compressed, frontal figure where the 'face' is formed by two inward-facing serpent heads at the neck. The skirt is a writhing mass of interlocked serpents. The necklace has alternating severed hands and hearts with a skull at the base. Clawed feet. Overall: a dense accumulation of death/earth symbols. Compose as a near-symmetrical tower of symbols.

deities.name: Xipe Totec

diagnostic_elements: wearing flayed human skin as a suit
flayed skin hands hanging over deity hands
square eye holes in flayed face mask (tzontecomatl)
red-mesh (nets of flesh) over skin surface
visual_attributes.mask: Flayed face worn as mask; squinting half-closed eyes showing through eye holes; mouth slightly open; skin wrinkled

deities.generative_hint: The outer surface is a flayed human skin worn like a costume. The flayed hands hang over the deity's real hands (four visible hands total). The face mask has squinting/half-closed eyes visible through the eye holes. Skin surface has a reddish mesh pattern. Think: a disturbing second skin, slightly too large for the body inside it.

deities.name: Itzamna

diagnostic_elements: elderly face with large hooked nose
flower or sun signs in headdress
associated with codices and writing instruments
visual_attributes.forms: May appear as cosmic iguana/crocodile (the sky itself), as an old man, or as a young lord

deities.generative_hint: Old beaked or large-nosed elderly face; large square eye with iris marking; often holding a codex or writing instrument. When depicted as cosmic creature: a large iguana or crested lizard whose body spans the sky horizon.

deities.name: Ix Chel

diagnostic_elements: inverted water jar pouring a flood
visual_attributes.forms: Old crone form with jaguar claws; also young beautiful moon goddess form

deities.generative_hint: Old woman form: elderly, clawed, with a serpent in her headdress and an upended water jar. Crosshatch skin marking = death/underworld association. The inverted water jar is the key visual: a vessel pouring water flood downward.

deities.name: Chaac

visual_attributes.architectural_use: Stacked Chac masks at corners of Puuc-style buildings; each mask is a profile Chac head built from individual modular stone blocks

deities.generative_hint: The nose is everything: a long downward-curving scroll hook, like a snout or proboscis extending from the face. Large circular eye. Often holding a stone axe (= lightning bolt). For Puuc architectural masks: assemble individual stone elements (the eye, nose, teeth, cheek) as separate modular pieces creating a stacked frontal face at building corners.

deities.name: Hunahpu / Maize God (Jun Ajaw / Ixim)

diagnostic_elements: elongated foliated head (head shaped like a maize ear with corn silk/tassel top)
maize plant emerging from cleft in head
visual_attributes.palenque_form: Resurrected from the underworld; kneeling in the earth with maize growing from his head; on Palenque sarcophagus lid

deities.generative_hint: The head is shaped like a maize cob — elongated, rounded at the top, with corn leaves/silk sprouting from the crown. Young handsome face. Often shown being resurrected, rising from a split turtle shell or from the cleft earth. Jade pectoral and ear ornaments.

deities.name: Cocijo

visual_attributes.ceramic_context: Primarily known from Zapotec funerary urns; mask appears as frontal face attachment

deities.generative_hint: Cocijo's most diagnostic feature is the downward-projecting bifurcated tongue — a Y-shaped forked element extending below the lower lip. Square open mouth. Circular large eyes. Often rendered in ceramic grey-buff tones (funerary urn context). Strictly frontal; rigidly symmetrical.
